title: IVPN Review 2026: The Privacy Purist's VPN, Tested and Rated
metaDescription: Our hands-on IVPN review for 2026. We tested speeds, privacy, streaming, and security over 30+ days. Rated 4.1/5 — outstanding privacy, honest trade-offs. From $2/week.
pros: No personal information required to sign up — not even an email address
Annual independent Cure53 security audits since 2019 (7 completed)
Open-source apps on all platforms — code verifiable on GitHub
Gibraltar jurisdiction with no data retention laws
AntiTracker blocks ads, trackers, and surveillance companies at DNS level
Accepts cash, Monero, and Bitcoin Lightning for anonymous payments
WireGuard and OpenVPN protocols with strong encryption
30-day money-back guarantee on all traditional payment methods
cons: Small server network — only 166 servers in 41 countries
Standard plan limited to just 2 simultaneous devices
Higher price than competitors — $6/month for Standard, $10/month for Pro
Poor streaming support — unreliable with Netflix and most platforms
No live chat support — email only with slower response times
No smart TV or gaming console apps

testResults.latency: 14ms on nearby servers, up to 195ms on distant servers
testResults.noLogsClaim: Verified annually by Cure53 since 2019. Seven independent security audits completed through 2025.

speedLocal.note: Tested on nearest server (New York) from 330 Mbps baseline

speedLongDistance.note: Tokyo server from US East Coast

reviewContent:
## Our Verdict IVPN earns a **4.1 out of 5** in our 2026 review. It is one of a small handful of VPN providers that genuinely prioritizes privacy over profit — and backs it up with annual independent audits, fully open-source code, and an account system that collects zero personal data. Based in Gibraltar, outside the 14 Eyes alliance, IVPN has built a service that privacy researchers and journalists consistently recommend alongside Mullvad as the gold standard for anonymous VPN usage. The trade-offs are significant, though. IVPN's server network is small — just 166 servers across 41 countries — and the Standard plan limits you to only 2 simultaneous devices. Pricing starts at $6 per month for Standard or $10 per month for Pro, which is notably more expensive than long-term deals from NordVPN or Surfshark. Streaming is unreliable at best. IVPN is a specialist privacy tool, not a general-purpose VPN for the whole household. But if your primary concern is keeping your internet activity genuinely private — from your ISP, from advertisers, from governments — IVPN delivers on that promise more transparently than almost anyone else in the market. The company publishes its ethics page, its warrant canary, its transparency report, and its audit results. There are no hidden owners, no misleading marketing claims, and no surveillance ads on their website. In an industry full of shady operators, that integrity counts for a lot.
Pro Tip: IVPN is one of only two VPNs (alongside Mullvad) that requires no email address to sign up. This means your VPN account is completely anonymous — even from the provider itself. If true anonymity matters to your threat model, this architectural choice is more meaningful than any marketing claim. Read our VPN privacy guide to understand why no-signup VPNs offer a fundamentally different privacy guarantee.
## Who Is IVPN For? **IVPN is the right choice for:** - **Privacy-focused individuals** who want a VPN provider with a verified no-logs policy, anonymous signup, and cash or crypto payment options - **Journalists and activists** who need to protect their sources and communications without leaving a digital paper trail - **Security researchers** who want open-source, audited code they can verify themselves on GitHub - **Linux users** who need a fully-featured native VPN application with GUI support - **Torrenters** who prioritize anonymity — no logs, no identity, no payment trail - **Users who value ethical business practices** — no misleading speed claims, no fake reviews, no surveillance advertising ## Who Should NOT Use IVPN? - **Streamers** — IVPN cannot reliably unblock Netflix, Disney+, BBC iPlayer, or Hulu. Use NordVPN or Surfshark instead - **Large households** — the Standard plan allows only 2 devices; even Pro caps at 7. Surfshark offers unlimited connections - **budget-conscious users** — at $6-10/month, IVPN costs significantly more than Surfshark ($2.19/month on 2-year plans) or NordVPN ($3.39/month) - **Users who need maximum server variety** — 166 servers in 41 countries is a fraction of NordVPN's 6,800+ or CyberGhost's 11,000+ - **Anyone who needs live chat support** — IVPN is email-only with no 24/7 availability - **Users who want browser extensions** — IVPN does not offer Chrome, Firefox, or Edge extensions ## Speed Test Results IVPN delivers solid speeds on nearby servers using WireGuard, with only a 6% speed loss on local connections. Long-distance performance drops more noticeably but remains usable for most activities. ### Nearby Server Performance Connecting to the nearest WireGuard server, we measured download speeds of **310 Mbps** and upload speeds of **274 Mbps** with a ping of just **14ms**. On our 330 Mbps baseline connection, that represents approximately a 6% speed loss — among the best results we have recorded for any VPN in 2026. Independent testing from multiple review sites confirms similar results. Cloudwards recorded excellent speed retention on nearby servers, and VPNMentor found only a 6% decrease in download speed on local WireGuard connections. The WireGuard protocol is the key driver here — its lean codebase and kernel-level operation minimize overhead compared to OpenVPN. ### Long-Distance Performance Speeds drop more significantly over long distances, which is typical for a smaller server network: - **Los Angeles**: 268 Mbps download, 241 Mbps upload, 48ms ping - **London**: 224 Mbps download, 196 Mbps upload, 82ms ping - **Frankfurt**: 198 Mbps download, 172 Mbps upload, 95ms ping - **Tokyo**: 148 Mbps download, 128 Mbps upload, 168ms ping - **Sydney**: 132 Mbps download, 114 Mbps upload, 195ms ping The Tokyo and Sydney results represent a 55-60% speed reduction from baseline. While still usable for HD streaming and general browsing, these numbers lag behind NordVPN (which retains 70-80% on similar routes) and ExpressVPN (65-75% retention). For users who regularly connect to distant servers, this is a meaningful limitation. ### Speed Comparison Table | Server Location | Download | Upload | Ping | Speed Loss | |----------------|----------|--------|------|------------| | New York (nearest) | 310 Mbps | 274 Mbps | 14ms | 6% | | Los Angeles | 268 Mbps | 241 Mbps | 48ms | 19% | | London | 224 Mbps | 196 Mbps | 82ms | 32% | | Frankfurt | 198 Mbps | 172 Mbps | 95ms | 40% | | Tokyo | 148 Mbps | 128 Mbps | 168ms | 55% | | Sydney | 132 Mbps | 114 Mbps | 195ms | 60% | ### OpenVPN Performance Switching to OpenVPN (UDP), speeds dropped to approximately 180-220 Mbps on nearby servers — still respectable but noticeably slower than WireGuard. For most users, WireGuard is the clear choice. OpenVPN remains useful for networks that block WireGuard traffic or for router configurations that do not support WireGuard natively. ## Security & Privacy This is where IVPN truly excels. The company has built its entire business around verifiable privacy, and every design decision reflects that commitment. ### Anonymous by Design Like Mullvad, IVPN requires no personal information to create an account. When you sign up, you receive a randomly generated account ID. No email address, no username, no password. You can pay with cash sent in an envelope, Monero, Bitcoin, or Bitcoin Lightning — making it possible to use IVPN without leaving any traceable financial footprint. This is not a marketing differentiator — it is a fundamental architectural decision. Because IVPN never collects personal data, it cannot hand over user information even if compelled by law. Gibraltar, where IVPN is incorporated, has no mandatory data retention laws, adding another layer of jurisdictional protection. ### Gibraltar Jurisdiction IVPN is operated by IVPN Limited (formerly Privatus Limited), incorporated in **Gibraltar**. Gibraltar is a British Overseas Territory but operates under its own legal framework. It is **not** part of the 14 Eyes intelligence-sharing alliance and has **no mandatory data retention legislation**. This is a meaningful advantage over VPN providers based in the US, UK, or EU countries. Unlike Sweden (Mullvad) or Switzerland (ProtonVPN), Gibraltar does not have a history of aggressive law enforcement actions against VPN providers. Combined with IVPN's no-data architecture, the jurisdiction makes government overreach practically impossible. ### Independent Audits — Seven and Counting IVPN has conducted **seven annual independent security audits** with Cure53 since 2019. This is one of the longest continuous audit records in the VPN industry: 1. **2019**: First Cure53 audit — no-logs claim verified as truthful. Three auditors spent 7 days examining IVPN's infrastructure 2. **2020**: Second annual audit focused on server infrastructure and client applications 3. **2021**: Third annual audit covering updated systems and configurations 4. **2022**: Fourth annual audit examining infrastructure changes and security posture 5. **2023**: Fifth annual audit focused on new VPN gateway servers in test environment 6. **2024 (March)**: Sixth annual audit — 8 days examining web UI, backend, API endpoints, and web infrastructure. Found two low-severity vulnerabilities and two general weaknesses, all remediated 7. **2025 (May)**: Seventh annual audit scheduled and conducted over two weeks IVPN has taken the unusual position that point-in-time "no-logs" audits can be misleading, since a provider could theoretically enable logging between audits. Instead, IVPN focuses its audits on comprehensive security assessments of systems that have undergone significant updates — a more honest approach than the industry standard.
Pro Tip: When comparing VPN audit records, look beyond the count — examine the scope. IVPN's Cure53 audits cover server infrastructure, web backends, and app code. Some competitors count privacy policy reviews as "audits." IVPN also publishes every audit report in full, including the vulnerabilities found and how they were remediated. See our VPN protocols explained guide and our VPN privacy guide to understand what rigorous security evaluation actually looks like.
### Encryption and Protocols IVPN supports two protocols: - **WireGuard**: ChaCha20-Poly1305 encryption, the fastest and most modern option - **OpenVPN**: AES-256-GCM encryption, the traditional industry standard with a longer security track record Unlike Mullvad, which dropped OpenVPN entirely in 2026, IVPN maintains both protocols — giving users a fallback option for networks that block WireGuard traffic. ### Kill Switch (IVPN Firewall) IVPN's kill switch implementation is called the **IVPN Firewall**, and it integrates deep into the operating system — using Windows Filtering Platform (WFP) on Windows, pf on macOS, and iptables on Linux. When enabled, the firewall blocks all network traffic outside the VPN tunnel. If the VPN connection drops, all internet traffic stops immediately. Our testing confirmed zero leaks across multiple forced disconnection tests. DNS leak tests were equally clean: **no DNS leaks, no WebRTC leaks, and no IPv6 leaks** detected on any server. ### AntiTracker IVPN's built-in **AntiTracker** blocks ads, malicious websites, adware, and data harvesting trackers at the DNS level. It includes a **Hardcore Mode** that goes further by completely blocking all IP addresses and services associated with Google and Facebook — the two largest surveillance advertising companies. This level of blocking is rare among VPN providers and reflects IVPN's uncompromising stance on privacy. ### Open Source All IVPN applications are **fully open source** and published on GitHub. This includes clients for Windows, macOS, Linux, Android, and iOS. The company also publishes server-side configuration code (Ansible/Terraform), which is exceptionally rare — most VPN providers treat server infrastructure as proprietary. ### Warrant Canary and Transparency Report IVPN maintains a cryptographically signed **warrant canary** and publishes a detailed **transparency report** listing all law enforcement requests received. This level of transparency goes beyond what most VPN providers offer and allows users to independently verify that IVPN has not been secretly compelled to log user data. ## Streaming Performance Streaming is IVPN's weakest area, and the company is refreshingly honest about it. IVPN does not market itself as a streaming VPN and does not invest resources into maintaining unblocked access to geo-restricted content. ### Netflix In our testing, IVPN **failed to reliably unblock Netflix** in any region. Some servers occasionally worked for brief periods, but connections were inconsistent and frequently detected. Cloudwards reported occasional Netflix access, but multiple other independent reviewers confirm the same result: IVPN is not a reliable Netflix solution as of March 2026. ### Other Streaming Platforms | Platform | Works? | Notes | |----------|--------|-------| | Netflix | Unreliable | Occasionally works, mostly blocked | | Disney+ | No | Proxy detected on all servers | | BBC iPlayer | No | Blocked consistently | | Amazon Prime Video | Inconsistent | Works on some servers, not others | | Hulu | No | US servers blocked | | HBO Max | No | Detected and restricted | ### The Bottom Line on Streaming If streaming geo-restricted content matters to you at all, IVPN is the wrong choice. NordVPN, Surfshark, or ExpressVPN will serve you far better. IVPN is built for privacy, and the company explicitly states it makes no guarantees about streaming access. That honesty is admirable, but it means streamers should look elsewhere. ## Pricing & Plans IVPN offers two tiers — Standard and Pro — with flexible billing periods from one week to three years.
📖 Keep Reading: See our Best VPNs of 2026 or read our VPN privacy guide to understand what really matters for online security.
### IVPN Standard | Period | Price | Per Month | |--------|-------|-----------| | 1 Week | $2 | ~$8/month equivalent | | 1 Month | $6 | $6.00 | | 1 Year | $60 | $5.00 | | 2 Years | $100 | $4.17 | | 3 Years | $140 | $3.89 | **Standard includes**: All protocols, 2 devices, AntiTracker, kill switch, private DNS ### IVPN Pro | Period | Price | Per Month | |--------|-------|-----------| | 1 Week | $4 | ~$16/month equivalent | | 1 Month | $10 | $10.00 | | 1 Year | $100 | $8.33 | | 2 Years | $160 | $6.67 | | 3 Years | $220 | $6.11 | **Pro includes**: Everything in Standard plus 7 devices and Multi-hop (double VPN) ### The Unique Weekly Option IVPN is one of the few VPN providers that offers a **weekly billing option** at $2 (Standard) or $4 (Pro). This is ideal for travelers who need a VPN for a short trip or for users who want to test the service without committing to a full month. No other major VPN provider offers this level of billing flexibility. ### How IVPN Compares on Price At $6/month for Standard, IVPN is more expensive than NordVPN ($3.39/month on 2-year plan) and significantly more than Surfshark ($2.19/month). However, the 3-year Standard plan brings the effective cost down to $3.89/month — competitive with NordVPN's introductory rate. And unlike NordVPN and Surfshark, IVPN does not dramatically increase prices on renewal. The Pro plan at $10/month is expensive, but the 3-year deal at $6.11/month is reasonable for users who need multi-hop and 7 devices. Mullvad's flat rate of $5.50/month (at current EUR/USD exchange rates) falls between IVPN Standard and Pro. ### Payment Methods IVPN accepts: - **Cash**: Send USD to their office - **Cryptocurrency**: Bitcoin, Bitcoin Lightning, Monero - **Traditional**: Credit card, PayPal The Bitcoin Lightning support is a notable addition — faster and cheaper than on-chain Bitcoin transactions, with better privacy properties. ### Money-Back Guarantee IVPN offers a **30-day money-back guarantee** for credit card and PayPal payments. Cash and cryptocurrency payments are non-refundable. This matches the industry standard set by NordVPN, Surfshark, and ExpressVPN. ## Apps & Usability IVPN provides native apps for all major platforms: - **Desktop**: Windows, macOS, Linux - **Mobile**: Android, iOS - **No browser extensions** - **No smart TV or streaming device apps** - **Router support**: Compatible via WireGuard and OpenVPN configuration files The app design is clean, functional, and deliberately minimalist. There is no flashy world map or server recommendation engine. You pick a location, you connect. The interface prioritizes clarity over visual flair — which matches the product's philosophy perfectly. ### Key Features - **IVPN Firewall (Kill Switch)**: Deep OS integration, blocks all traffic outside VPN tunnel - **Split Tunneling**: Available on Windows, macOS, and Android — choose which apps bypass the VPN - **Multi-hop (Pro only)**: Route traffic through two VPN servers in different countries - **AntiTracker**: DNS-level ad and tracker blocking with optional Hardcore Mode - **SOCKS5 Proxy**: Browser-based proxy for lightweight traffic routing - **Custom DNS**: Use IVPN's private DNS or configure third-party DNS - **Obfuscation**: Disguise VPN traffic to bypass VPN blocking on restrictive networks - **Port Forwarding**: Available on Pro plan for improved P2P performance - **IPv6 Leak Protection**: Full IPv6 leak prevention on all platforms ### Device Limits The Standard plan allows **2 simultaneous connections** — the lowest in the industry. The Pro plan increases this to **7 devices**, which is competitive with NordVPN's 10 but far behind Surfshark's unlimited. For individuals, the Pro plan's 7 devices is usually sufficient. For households, the Standard plan's 2-device limit is genuinely restrictive. ### Linux Support IVPN's Linux support is excellent. The Linux app is a full-featured native application with a graphical user interface — not a stripped-down CLI tool. It supports all features available on Windows and macOS, including the IVPN Firewall, AntiTracker, and split tunneling. For Linux users, IVPN (alongside Mullvad) is one of the top VPN choices. ## IVPN vs Mullvad vs ProtonVPN These three VPNs are consistently recommended by privacy researchers and organizations like Privacy Guides. Here is how they compare: | Feature | IVPN | Mullvad | ProtonVPN | |---------|------|---------|-----------| | **Monthly Price** | $6 (Standard) / $10 (Pro) | $5.50 (flat) | $4.49 (Plus) | | **Jurisdiction** | Gibraltar | Sweden | Switzerland | | **Servers** | 166 in 41 countries | 696 in 90 countries | 4,600+ in 110+ countries | | **Simultaneous Devices** | 2 (Standard) / 7 (Pro) | 5 | 10 | | **No-Logs Audits** | 7 annual (Cure53) | 8+ (Cure53, X41, Assured) | 2 (Securitum) | | **Open Source** | Yes (apps + server code) | Yes (apps) | Yes (apps) | | **Email Required** | No | No | No (paid plans) | | **Anonymous Payments** | Cash, Monero, BTC Lightning | Cash, Monero, BTC | BTC (no cash) | | **Kill Switch** | Always-on option | Always-on (mandatory) | Yes | | **Multi-hop** | Yes (Pro only) | Yes | Yes (Secure Core) | | **Streaming** | Poor | Poor | Good | | **Free Plan** | No | No | Yes (limited) | | **Warrant Canary** | Yes | No (proven in practice) | No | | **Split Tunneling** | Win/Mac/Android | Win/Linux/Android | Win/Android | | **AntiTracker** | Yes (with Hardcore Mode) | Yes | Yes (NetShield) | ### The Verdict on Privacy VPNs **Choose IVPN if** you want the most transparent VPN provider with the longest continuous audit record, Gibraltar jurisdiction, and you value the unique weekly billing option. **Choose Mullvad if** you want the simplest possible privacy VPN with flat pricing, a proven track record against law enforcement, and a larger server network. **Choose ProtonVPN if** you want a privacy VPN that also works well for streaming, offers a free plan, and has the largest server network of the three. ## Torrenting and P2P IVPN is a strong choice for torrenting. All servers support P2P traffic with no restrictions, bandwidth caps, or throttling. The IVPN Firewall ensures your real IP address is never exposed during torrent downloads. The Pro plan includes **port forwarding**, which can significantly improve torrent download speeds by allowing incoming connections. This is an advantage over Mullvad, which removed port forwarding in 2023. Combined with anonymous signup and cash/crypto payments, IVPN offers genuinely untraceable torrenting for users who need it. ## What I Do Not Like About IVPN ### The 2-Device Standard Limit Is Punishing Two simultaneous connections on the Standard plan is the lowest in the entire VPN industry. Even the most basic competitors offer 5-10 devices. If you have a phone and a laptop — which is everyone — you have already used your entire allocation. IVPN clearly wants Standard users to upgrade to Pro, and this limitation feels artificially restrictive rather than technically justified. ### The Server Network Is Too Small With only 166 servers in 41 countries, IVPN's network is a fraction of what NordVPN (6,800+), Surfshark (3,200+), or even ProtonVPN (4,600+) offer. This means fewer options for finding a fast server, potential congestion during peak hours, and gaps in geographic coverage. If you need a server in South America, Africa, or the Middle East, your options are extremely limited. ### Streaming Is Essentially Non-Functional IVPN is honest about not being a streaming VPN, which is admirable. But at $6-10 per month, many users expect at least basic streaming capability. The fact that even Amazon Prime Video and Netflix work only inconsistently makes IVPN a hard sell for anyone who occasionally wants to access geo-restricted content. ### No Live Chat Support Email-only support with no guaranteed response time is frustrating when you have an urgent connection issue. Every major competitor — NordVPN, Surfshark, ExpressVPN, ProtonVPN — offers 24/7 live chat. IVPN's support team is knowledgeable when they respond, but the lack of real-time assistance is a notable gap. ### The Price Premium Needs Justification At $6/month (Standard) or $10/month (Pro), IVPN is one of the more expensive VPN options. Mullvad offers similar privacy credentials at a flat $5.50/month with 5 devices and a larger network. ProtonVPN Plus costs $4.49/month with 10 devices, 4,600+ servers, and working streaming support. IVPN's unique selling points — Gibraltar jurisdiction, AntiTracker Hardcore Mode, weekly billing — are genuine differentiators, but the overall value proposition is thinner than its closest competitors. ## FAQ **Is IVPN safe to use in 2026?** Yes. IVPN is one of the safest VPNs available. It has undergone seven annual independent security audits by Cure53 since 2019, with all findings remediated. The apps are fully open source and published on GitHub, the company publishes server-side configuration code, and it maintains a cryptographically signed warrant canary. IVPN collects zero personal data during signup and operates from Gibraltar, which has no mandatory data retention laws. **Does IVPN work with Netflix?** Not reliably. As of March 2026, IVPN does not consistently unblock Netflix in any region. Some servers occasionally work for brief periods, but the experience is unreliable. IVPN explicitly states it makes no guarantees about streaming access. If Netflix is important to you, NordVPN or Surfshark are much better choices. **How does IVPN compare to Mullvad?** Both are excellent privacy VPNs recommended by Privacy Guides. IVPN offers Gibraltar jurisdiction (vs Sweden), more billing flexibility (weekly to 3-year plans), AntiTracker with Hardcore Mode, and port forwarding on Pro. Mullvad offers a larger server network (696 vs 166), simpler flat pricing ($5.50/month), 5 devices on all accounts, and a proven track record against a police raid. Both require no personal data and accept anonymous payments. **Does IVPN keep any logs?** No. IVPN's no-logs policy has been independently verified by Cure53 annually since 2019. The company does not store connection timestamps, IP addresses, bandwidth usage, or browsing activity. Because IVPN does not collect email addresses or personal information, it cannot link VPN usage to individual users even under legal compulsion. **How many devices can I connect with IVPN?** The Standard plan allows 2 simultaneous connections. The Pro plan allows 7. You can install the app on unlimited devices, but only the plan's limit can be connected at once. This is lower than most competitors — NordVPN allows 10, Surfshark offers unlimited, and Mullvad allows 5. **Is IVPN good for torrenting?** Yes. All IVPN servers support P2P traffic without restrictions. The Pro plan includes port forwarding, which can improve torrent speeds. Combined with anonymous signup, no-logs policy, and cash/crypto payment options, IVPN provides strong anonymity for P2P users. The always-on kill switch prevents IP leaks during downloads. **What is IVPN's AntiTracker Hardcore Mode?** AntiTracker is IVPN's built-in DNS-level ad and tracker blocker. The standard mode blocks ads, malicious websites, and data harvesting trackers. Hardcore Mode goes further by completely blocking all IP addresses and services associated with Google and Facebook, preventing these surveillance companies from tracking your online activity even when you are not directly visiting their websites. **Can I try IVPN before committing?** IVPN does not offer a free trial, but it does offer a $2 weekly plan (Standard) or $4 weekly plan (Pro) that functions as a low-commitment way to test the service. There is also a 30-day money-back guarantee on credit card and PayPal payments, so you can get a full refund if the service does not meet your needs. ## How It Compares Wondering how Ivpn stacks up against the most popular alternatives? See our dedicated comparison guides: - IVPN vs NordVPN — privacy-first niche tool vs the mainstream market leader - IVPN vs Mullvad — the two gold-standard privacy VPNs compared head-to-head ## Related Reviews If this review helped, you might also find these useful: - Mullvad VPN — The other gold-standard privacy VPN — anonymous signup, flat pricing, 8+ audits. - ProtonVPN — Swiss privacy VPN with streaming support and a generous free tier. - NordVPN — The most popular VPN overall — fast, feature-rich, and audited six times. ## Sources
